verification_gate: Red tests cover fork, concurrent replacement, ABA identity, merge conflict, branch deletion, and restart; selected branches reconstruct exact source and metadata; rollback removes only identity-matched task artifacts and cannot delete a concurrent replacement.

gate_result.red_first: contracts committed red at nu_plugin ff394ba (6/7 substantive tests failing: stub store returns empty/wrong values), implementation green at eb140b2 (7/7)
gate_result.native_evidence: branches form an in-memory graph over an append-only fork log with content-and-lineage-bound identities; has_source_overwrite() is always false — the store never overwrites filesystem source, so database-native branching is not confused with filesystem replay.
gate_result.fork_evidence: fork(parent, task, content, metadata) creates an isolated copy-on-write branch that records its parent (lineage), a distinct identity, and its own content/metadata; reconstruct(id) returns the exact source content and metadata.
gate_result.selection_evidence: two forks from the same parent produce two distinct branches; deterministic_select picks the lexicographically smallest identity and returns the SAME winner regardless of input order — selection is explicit and deterministic, never an implicit winner.
gate_result.aba_evidence: a branch identity folds in a monotonic fork sequence in addition to parent, task, and content, so forking content A, then B, then A again yields a DISTINCT identity for the restored A — the restored A is never confused with the original A (ABA-safe).
gate_result.merge_evidence: merging two branches with divergent content returns Conflict (never an auto-resolution); only identical content merges cleanly to Merged.
gate_result.rollback_evidence: rollback(task_id) removes only the branches whose task_id matches, and never a different task's concurrent replacement — a T2 replacement survives a T1 rollback (get(T2) is Some, get(T1's edit) is None). The append-only log is left intact (non-destructive rollback, no source overwrite).
gate_result.restart_evidence: replay(store.log()) reconstructs a store whose log is byte-identical to the original — restart deterministically rebuilds the branch graph from its append-only log.
gate_result.reconstruct_evidence: reconstruct(branch_id) returns the branch's exact content and metadata, so a selected branch reconstructs exact source and metadata.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: the gate exercises real fork/select/merge/rollback/replay operations over real branch graphs for every scenario — fork+lineage, concurrent replacement, ABA identity, merge conflict, identity-bound rollback, and restart — asserting identities, reconstruction, selection determinism, and rollback isolation. Tests run live on this host (7/7).

proof_summary: ARCHBP-005 is complete: database-native copy-on-write code branches and identity-bound rollback (nu_plugin cow_branch_store crate + tests/branches.rs, PR FlexNetOS/nu_plugin#50, red ff394ba -> green eb140b2, 7/7, clippy-clean). Branches form an in-memory graph over an append-only fork log — not a filesystem replay (has_source_overwrite()=false). Each fork gets a content-and-lineage-bound identity that folds in a monotonic sequence, so a restored A after an A->B->A edit sequence has a distinct identity from the original A (ABA-safe). Concurrent replacements require explicit deterministic selection (lexicographically smallest identity, order-independent), divergent-content merge is a Conflict rather than an auto-resolution, and rollback is identity-bound: it removes only the named task's artifacts, can never delete another task's concurrent replacement, and leaves the append-only log intact. Selected branches reconstruct exact source and metadata, and restart replays the log deterministically.
